title: iva frutescens
intro: Iva frutescens, also known as marshelder or elder mallow, is a perennial herb native to North America, characterized by its white or pale pink flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to the presence of saponins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Iva frutescens include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support the digestive system, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. Iva frutescens can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
